text: Except for indemnity obligations and breaches of confidentiality, each party's aggregate liability under this Agreement shall not exceed the fees paid or payable in the twelve (12) months preceding the claim.
Neither party may assign this Order Form without the other party's prior written consent, except to an affiliate or in connection with a merger or sale of substantially all assets.

If extraordinary events outside a party's reasonable control (including natural disasters or government actions) halt performance for a prolonged period, the affected party's duties pause. If the halt exceeds three months, the parties shall negotiate in good faith; failing agreement, termination may occur with one month's notice.

Termination for Cause. Either party may terminate immediately if the other party commits a material breach and fails to cure within ten (10) days after written notice. Termination for Cause is not available for convenience and does not require the notice periods applicable to convenience termination.

Force Majeure. Suspension lasts only while the event continues. If performance is blocked for more than fourteen (14) days, the non-affected party may terminate immediately without further liability except for amounts already due.
Recipient shall protect Discloser's Confidential Information using the same degree of care it uses for its own similar information, but not less than reasonable care.

Termination for Convenience. Customer may terminate this Agreement for convenience at any time without cause upon thirty (30) days' written notice and payment of fees earned through the effective date of termination.

Section 9 — Indemnification. Vendor shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Customer from third-party claims alleging infringement of intellectual property arising from the Deliverables, except to the extent caused by Customer modifications.
This Agreement is governed by the laws of the State of Delaware, without regard to conflict-of-laws principles. Venue shall lie exclusively in Wilmington.

Either party may terminate this Statement of Work for convenience upon not less than sixty (60) days' prior written notice. For termination for material breach, the non-breaching party may terminate immediately if the breach is not cured within fifteen (15) days of written notice.

Initial term is twelve (12) months. Unless either party provides written notice of non-renewal at least forty-five (45) days before the end of the then-current term, this Order Form automatically renews for successive one-year periods.
Invoices are due Net 30. Late payments accrue interest at 1.5% per month or the maximum permitted by law, whichever is lower.

Section 12 — Force Majeure. If a Force Majeure Event prevents a party from performing for more than ninety (90) continuous days, either party may terminate this Agreement upon thirty (30) days' prior written notice. For events under ninety (90) days, obligations are suspended only for the duration of the event.

Article VIII — Force Majeure. Neither party is liable for delay caused by events beyond reasonable control, including strikes, embargoes, or power outages. This clause does not specify a numeric duration for suspension or termination.
Tenant acknowledges the rules in Exhibit B. Force Majeure is defined in Section 14; however, rent abatement is addressed separately in Section 5 and may apply even when Section 14 is implicated.
